Home / Lore / Locations / Washington, D.C.

location

Washington, D.C.

The primary setting of The Division 2, where institutional collapse, civilian reconstruction and competing factions converge around the U.S. capital.

Connectionnot applicable Continuitydivision game universe Canon scopegame canon Verificationsource reviewed Reviewed2026-07-19

Capital in crisis

Washington is not just a backdrop: the failure of the SHD Network and the struggle over federal institutions make the city the strategic center of The Division 2's opening campaign. [1][2]

Territory and reconstruction

The city's neighborhoods, control points and settlements express the continuing conflict between hostile factions, SHD Agents and organized civilians. [1][3]

Claim ledger

Every factual statement below must retain at least one traceable source.

  1. confirmed

    The official story premise places Washington on the brink of collapse amid lawlessness, instability and rumours of a Capitol coup. [1]

  2. confirmed

    Several months after the first game's events, Agents across the country are summoned to Washington after the nationwide Division Network goes down. [2]

  3. confirmed

    Ubisoft presents Washington as contested by hostile factions and defended through Division operations and civilian settlements. [1][3]

Sources

  1. [1] The Division 2 Story & Factions — Ubisoft (undated). Accessed 2026-07-19.
  2. [2] The Division 2 – State of the Game Recap: The Story So Far — Ubisoft News (2020-02-05). Accessed 2026-07-19.
  3. [3] Endgame Specializations, Black Tusks, and The Fight to Reclaim DC — Ubisoft (2019-02-04). Accessed 2026-07-19.
Editorial status: District-by-district pages and landmark histories remain a later inventory task.

Unofficial, source-led research into The Division universe and its real-world influences.
